3187	\begin{lemma}
3188	[The slice-to-geometric isomorphism in pullback form (gap1, C, step 4')]
3189	  \label{lem:over_restrict_pullback_iso}
3190	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.overRestrictPullbackIso}
3191	  \uses{lem:over_restrict_iso, lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib,
3192	    lem:modules_pullback_mathlib}
3193	  \textit{The pullback packaging of the slice-touching bridge; the form consumed by the P1 transport.}
3194	  With \(X\) and \(U \subseteq X\) as above and \(M\) a sheaf of modules on \(X\), the transport of the
3195	  abstract Grothendieck-slice restriction \(M.\mathrm{over}\,U\) under the step-3 equivalence functor
3196	  (\cref{def:over_restrict_equiv}) is canonically isomorphic to the inverse-image (pullback) of \(M\)
3197	  along the open immersion \(U.\iota\):
3198	  \[
3199	    (\mathrm{overRestrictEquiv}\,U).\mathrm{functor}.\mathrm{obj}\,(M.\mathrm{over}\,U)
3200	      \;\cong\; (U.\iota^{*})\,M
3201	  \]
3202	  (\cref{lem:modules_pullback_mathlib}). This is the pullback packaging of the slice-touching bridge
3203	  \cref{lem:over_restrict_iso}; it is exactly the form in which a presentation of
3204	  \(M.\mathrm{over}\,U\) is transported into a presentation of the geometric pullback \(U.\iota^{*}M\),
3205	  and it is the ingredient consumed by the per-element local-tilde transport
3206	  \cref{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent}.
3207	\end{lemma}
3208	\begin{proof}
3209	  \uses{lem:over_restrict_iso, lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib}
3210	  Compose the slice-to-geometric isomorphism \cref{lem:over_restrict_iso}, which identifies the
3211	  transported slice restriction with the geometric restriction
3212	  \((\mathrm{restrictFunctor}\,U.\iota).\mathrm{obj}\,M\), with the component at \(M\) of the natural
3213	  isomorphism \(\mathrm{restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\,U.\iota\)
3214	  (\cref{lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib}) identifying the geometric restriction with
3215	  the pullback \(U.\iota^{*}M\).
3216	\end{proof}

3220	\begin{lemma}[Quasi-coherent restricts to a tilde on each basic open of the cover (gap1, P1)]
3221	  \label{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent}
3222	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isIso_fromTildeΓ_restrict_basicOpen}
3223	  \uses{lem:over_restrict_iso, lem:over_restrict_pullback_iso, lem:presentation_map_mathlib,
3224	    lem:quasicoherentData_bind_mathlib, lem:modules_pullback_mathlib,
3225	    lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation_mathlib, lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib,
3226	    lem:exists_finite_basicOpen_cover_le_quasicoherentData}
3227	  % NOTE: the pinned Lean decl isIso_fromTildeΓ_restrict_basicOpen does NOT yet exist; it is the
3228	  % per-element transport step of gap1. It replaces the former lem:exists_isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_cover,
3229	  % whose \lean{} pin named a non-existent decl (and whose Mayer--Vietoris framing is superseded by
3230	  % the section-localization descent of lem:section_localization_descent).
3231	  \textit{Per-element step: on each basic open of the refined cover, the restricted sheaf is a
3232	  geometric tilde.}
3233	  Let \(M\) be a sheaf of modules on \(\Spec R\) with quasi-coherence data \(q\), and let \(r \in R\)
3234	  be such that \(D(r) \le q.X_i\) for some member of the cover (as produced by
3235	  \cref{lem:exists_finite_basicOpen_cover_le_quasicoherentData}). Then the restricted counit is an
3236	  isomorphism,
3237	  \[
3238	    \mathrm{IsIso}\bigl((M|_{D(r)}).\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}\bigr),
3239	  \]
3240	  i.e. \(M|_{D(r)}\) is identified with the tilde \(\widetilde{N}\) of the \(R_r\)-module
3241	  \(N = \Gamma(M, D(r))\) on \(\Spec R_r \cong D(r)\).
3242	\end{lemma}
3243	\begin{proof}
3244	  \uses{lem:over_restrict_iso, lem:over_restrict_pullback_iso, lem:presentation_map_mathlib,
3245	    lem:quasicoherentData_bind_mathlib, lem:modules_pullback_mathlib,
3246	    lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation_mathlib, lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib}
3247	  The quasi-coherence datum supplies a presentation
3248	  \(q.\mathrm{presentation}\,i : (M.\mathrm{over}\,(q.X_i)).\mathrm{Presentation}\) on the slice over
3249	  \(q.X_i\). Restrict it along the inclusion of opens \(D(r) \subseteq q.X_i\) by transporting across
3250	  the iterated Grothendieck slice — the template is \(\mathrm{QuasicoherentData.bind}\)
3251	  (\cref{lem:quasicoherentData_bind_mathlib}) — using \(\mathrm{Presentation.map}\)
3252	  (\cref{lem:presentation_map_mathlib}) along the colimit-preserving slice functor; this yields a
3253	  presentation of \(M.\mathrm{over}\,(D(r))\). Pushing through the slice-to-geometric bridge
3254	  \cref{lem:over_restrict_iso} converts it into a presentation of the geometric restriction
3255	  \((\mathrm{restrictFunctor}\,(D(r)).\iota).\mathrm{obj}\,M\); then, across the affine
3256	  identification \(D(r) \cong \Spec R_r\) (\cref{lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib}, by
3257	  \(\mathrm{Presentation.map}\) along the pullback functor \cref{lem:modules_pullback_mathlib}), into
3258	  a genuine global presentation of \(M|_{D(r)}\) as a sheaf of modules on \(\Spec R_r\). A global
3259	  presentation forces the \(\widetilde{(-)}\)-counit to be an isomorphism
3260	  (\cref{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation_mathlib}), giving
3261	  \(\mathrm{IsIso}\bigl((M|_{D(r)}).\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}\bigr)\).
3262	\end{proof}

3266	\begin{lemma}[Section-localization descent from a local-tilde cover (gap1 keystone, D)]
3267	  \label{lem:section_localization_descent}
3268	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent}
3269	  \uses{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent,
3270	    lem:exists_finite_basicOpen_cover_le_quasicoherentData, lem:existsUnique_gluing_mathlib,
3271	    lem:isLimitPullbackCone_mathlib, lem:isLocalization_flat_mathlib,
3272	    lem:isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict}
3273	  % NOTE: the pinned Lean decl isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent does NOT yet exist; it is the
3274	  % genuine new content of gap1 and is reused by gap2's section-localization characterization.
3275	  % isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_presentation does NOT close it: that lemma needs a GLOBAL
3276	  % presentation of M, whereas here only per-D(r) tilde data is available.
3277	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], "Properties of Schemes", \S "Sections over principal opens",
3278	  % Lemma \texttt{lemma-invert-f-sections} (read from references/stacks-properties.tex, L2152-L2170).
3279	  % Cf. [Hartshorne], II.5.3.
3280	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
3281	  % "Let $X$ be a scheme. Let $f \in \Gamma(X, \mathcal{O}_X)$.
3282	  %  Denote $X_f \subset X$ the open where $f$ is invertible, see
3283	  %  Schemes, Lemma \ref{schemes-lemma-f-open}.
3284	  %  If $X$ is quasi-compact and quasi-separated, the canonical map
3285	  %  $$
3286	  %  \Gamma(X, \mathcal{O}_X)_f \longrightarrow \Gamma(X_f, \mathcal{O}_X)
3287	  %  $$
3288	  %  is an isomorphism. Moreover, if $\mathcal{F}$ is a quasi-coherent
3289	  %  sheaf of $\mathcal{O}_X$-modules the map
3290	  %  $$
3291	  %  \Gamma(X, \mathcal{F})_f \longrightarrow \Gamma(X_f, \mathcal{F})
3292	  %  $$
3293	  %  is an isomorphism."
3294	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], "Properties of Schemes", \S ``Sections over principal opens'',
3295	  Lemma \texttt{lemma-invert-f-sections}; cf.\ [Hartshorne], II.5.3.}
3296	  Let \(M\) be a quasi-coherent sheaf of modules on \(\Spec R\) (equivalently, by
3297	  \cref{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent} and
3298	  \cref{lem:exists_finite_basicOpen_cover_le_quasicoherentData}, \(M\) is locally isomorphic to a
3299	  tilde \(\widetilde{N_j}\) on the members of a finite basic-open cover \(\{D(r_j)\}\) of
3300	  \(\Spec R\)). Then for every \(f \in R\) the section restriction
3301	  \(\Gamma(M, \top) \to \Gamma(M, D(f))\) exhibits the target as the localized module
3302	  \((\mathrm{powers}\,f)^{-1}\,\Gamma(M, \top)\); that is, it is
3303	  \(\mathrm{IsLocalizedModule}(\mathrm{powers}\,f)\) over \(R\).
3304	\end{lemma}
3305	% NOTE: the lemma STATEMENT is cited to Stacks lemma-invert-f-sections / Hartshorne II.5.3; the proof
3306	% below is a project-internal descent (finite sheaf equalizer over the cover + flatness of
3307	% localization) that deliberately does NOT route through the global affine equivalence (which is
3308	% gap1 itself), so there is no transcribed `% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF`.
3309	\begin{proof}
3310	  \uses{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent,
3311	    lem:exists_finite_basicOpen_cover_le_quasicoherentData, lem:existsUnique_gluing_mathlib,
3312	    lem:isLimitPullbackCone_mathlib, lem:isLocalization_flat_mathlib,
3313	    lem:isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict}
3314	  Refine the quasi-coherence cover to a finite basic-open cover \(\{D(r_j)\}_{j=1}^m\) of \(\Spec R\)
3315	  with each \(D(r_j) \le q.X_{i(j)}\)
3316	  (\cref{lem:exists_finite_basicOpen_cover_le_quasicoherentData}). On each \(D(r_j)\) the restricted
3317	  counit is an isomorphism (\cref{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent}), so
3318	  \(M|_{D(r_j)} \cong \widetilde{N_j}\) with \(N_j = \Gamma(M, D(r_j))\) a module over \(R_{r_j}\),
3319	  and the basic-open sections of \(M\) below \(D(r_j)\) are the corresponding module localizations
3320	  (\cref{lem:isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict}).
3321	
3322	  Fix \(f \in R\). The sheaf condition for \(M\) over the finite cover \(\{D(r_j)\}\) and its pairwise
3323	  intersections presents the global sections as the finite equalizer
3324	  \[
3325	    \Gamma(M, \top) \;=\; \mathrm{eq}\Bigl(\textstyle\prod_j \Gamma(M, D(r_j))
3326	      \;\rightrightarrows\; \prod_{j,k} \Gamma(M, D(r_jr_k))\Bigr),
3327	  \]
3328	  the Mayer--Vietoris / unique-gluing description of sections
3329	  (\cref{lem:isLimitPullbackCone_mathlib}, \cref{lem:existsUnique_gluing_mathlib}); the same holds
3330	  with \(\top\) replaced by \(D(f)\) and the cover by \(\{D(fr_j)\}\). Localizing at
3331	  \(\mathrm{powers}\,f\) is exact and commutes with this finite limit because localization is flat
3332	  (\cref{lem:isLocalization_flat_mathlib}). On each cover piece the localization of
3333	  \(\Gamma(M, D(r_j))\) away from \(f\) is \(\Gamma(M, D(fr_j)) = \Gamma(M|_{D(r_j)}, D(f))\), a
3334	  localization of the tilde \(\widetilde{N_j}\) computed by
3335	  \cref{lem:isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict}; the same on the overlaps. Hence the localized equalizer
3336	  computes \(\Gamma(M, D(f))\), and the comparison map
3337	  \((\mathrm{powers}\,f)^{-1}\,\Gamma(M, \top) \to \Gamma(M, D(f))\) is an isomorphism: the section
3338	  restriction is \(\mathrm{IsLocalizedModule}(\mathrm{powers}\,f)\) over \(R\). This is the
3339	  \(\Gamma(X, \mathcal{F})_f \cong \Gamma(X_f, \mathcal{F})\) of Stacks
3340	  \texttt{lemma-invert-f-sections} on \(X = \Spec R\), \(X_f = D(f)\), obtained directly from
3341	  local-tilde data without appeal to the global affine equivalence.
3342	\end{proof}

3344	\begin{lemma}[Quasi-coherent on an affine has trivial $\widetilde{(-)}$-counit (gap1)]
3345	  \label{lem:qcoh_affine_isIso_fromTildeΓ}
3346	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isQuasicoherent}
3347	  \uses{lem:section_localization_descent, lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff_isLocalizedModule_restrict}
3348	  % NOTE: the Lean decl does NOT yet exist. gap1 is the PRIMARY stated gap of the Quot-foundations
3349	  % layer — the QCoh(Spec R) ≃ Mod R essential-image gap (Stacks lemma-quasi-coherent-affine). The
3350	  % assembly is now a one-liner: the genuine content lives in the section-localization descent
3351	  % (\cref{lem:section_localization_descent}, keystone D), which is fed into the in-file equivalence
3352	  % \cref{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff_isLocalizedModule_restrict} (already axiom-clean). The descent (D)
3353	  % in turn rests on the slice-to-geometric bridge (\cref{lem:over_restrict_iso}, C) and the
3354	  % per-element local-tilde transport (\cref{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent}, P1).
3355	  % Route: analogies/quot-gap1-transport.md.
3356	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], "Schemes", Lemma \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine}
3357	  % (read from references/stacks-schemes.tex, L1279--1284).
3358	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $(X, \mathcal{O}_X) = (\Spec(R), \mathcal{O}_{\Spec(R)})$
3359	  % be an affine scheme. Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module. Then
3360	  % $\mathcal{F}$ is isomorphic to the sheaf associated to the $R$-module $\Gamma(X,
3361	  % \mathcal{F})$."
3362	  \textit{The affine equivalence QCoh\((\Spec R)\) \(\simeq\) Mod\((R)\): a quasi-coherent module
3363	  sheaf on \(\Spec R\) is \(\widetilde{\Gamma(M)}\) up to canonical isomorphism.}
3364	  Let \(M\) be a sheaf of modules on \(\Spec R\) that is quasi-coherent
3365	  (\(\mathrm{IsQuasicoherent}\,M\)). Then the counit \(M.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}\) is an isomorphism;
3366	  equivalently \(M\) lies in the essential image of \(\widetilde{(-)} : \mathrm{Mod}(R) \to (\Spec
3367	  R).\mathrm{Modules}\).
3368	\end{lemma}
3369	% NOTE: the proof is a one-line assembly — feed the keystone descent (D) into the in-file
3370	% equivalence isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff_isLocalizedModule_restrict — so it routes through no transcribed
3371	% Stacks gluing proof; the SOURCE QUOTE above cites the lemma STATEMENT (Stacks
3372	% lemma-quasi-coherent-affine).
3373	\begin{proof}
3374	  \uses{lem:section_localization_descent, lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff_isLocalizedModule_restrict}
3375	  Since \(M\) is quasi-coherent, the section-localization descent
3376	  (\cref{lem:section_localization_descent}) gives that for every \(f \in R\) the section restriction
3377	  \(\Gamma(M, \top) \to \Gamma(M, D(f))\) is \(\mathrm{IsLocalizedModule}(\mathrm{powers}\,f)\) over
3378	  \(R\). This is exactly the right-hand side of the equivalence
3379	  \cref{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff_isLocalizedModule_restrict} (already established in-file), whose
3380	  left-hand side is \(\mathrm{IsIso}\,M.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}\). Hence the counit is an isomorphism and
3381	  \(M\) is identified with \(\widetilde{\Gamma(M, \top)}\), placing \(M\) in the essential image of
3382	  \(\widetilde{(-)}\).
3383	\end{proof}

3385	\subsection{G1-assemble: from per-basic-open section localization to the
3386	\texorpdfstring{$\widetilde{(-)}$}{tilde}-counit}
3387	
3388	This subsection records the bridge \(\text{section-localization on every basic open} \Rightarrow
3389	\mathrm{IsIso}\,M.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}\): given that the section restriction localizes on every basic
3390	open, the tilde-Gamma counit is an isomorphism. Together with its converse
3391	\cref{lem:isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ} it assembles the equivalence
3392	\cref{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff_isLocalizedModule_restrict}, which is exactly what makes G1-core
3393	(\cref{lem:qcoh_affine_section_localization}) and gap1
3394	(\cref{lem:qcoh_affine_isIso_fromTildeΓ}) interderivable -- the route by which G1-core is obtained as
3395	a corollary of gap1. The supporting Mathlib facts are recorded as dependency anchors.

3447	  \label{lem:bijective_comp_of_localizations}
3448	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.bijective_comp_of_localizations}

3451	  \uses{lem:isLocalizedModule_linearEquiv_mathlib, lem:isLocalizedModule_linearMap_ext_mathlib}
3452	  \textit{Linear-algebra glue for the G1-assemble step.}
3453	  Let \(S \subseteq R\) be a submonoid and let \(g : M \to M'\), \(g' : M \to M''\) both exhibit a
3454	  localization of \(M\) at \(S\). If a linear map \(h : M' \to M''\) satisfies \(h \circ g = g'\),
3455	  then \(h\) is bijective.

3462	  coincide by uniqueness of maps out of a localization
3463	  (\cref{lem:isLocalizedModule_linearMap_ext_mathlib}). A linear equivalence is bijective, hence so
3464	  is \(h\).

3467	\begin{lemma}\leanok
3468	[Basis-wise isomorphism of sheaves on $\Spec R$]

3476	  Let \(\alpha : F \to G\) be a morphism of sheaves of \(R\)-modules on \(\Spec R\) whose component

3492	(G1-assemble)]
3493	  \label{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isLocalizedModule_restrict}
3494	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isLocalizedModule_restrict}
3495	  \uses{lem:isIso_sheaf_of_isIso_app_basicOpen, lem:bijective_comp_of_localizations,
3496	    lem:isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict}
3497	  \textit{The G1-assemble reduction: section-localization on every basic open forces the
3498	  \(\widetilde{(-)}\)-counit to be an isomorphism.}
3499	  Let \(M\) be a sheaf of modules on \(\Spec R\), and suppose that for every \(f \in R\) the section
3500	  restriction \(\Gamma(M, \top) \to \Gamma(M, D(f))\) exhibits the target as
3501	  \(\mathrm{IsLocalizedModule}(\mathrm{powers}\,f)\) over \(R\) -- exactly the conclusion of G1-core
3502	  (\cref{lem:qcoh_affine_section_localization}). Then the counit \(M.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}\) is an
3503	  isomorphism.
3504	\end{lemma}
3505	\begin{proof}

=== analogies/quot-gap1-transport.md ===
# Analogy: transporting local quasi-coherent presentations to finish gap1 on `Spec R`

## Mode
api-alignment

## Slug
quot-transport

## Iteration
030

## Question
To finish gap1 (`IsIso M.fromTildeΓ` for `M : (Spec R).Modules` with `[M.IsQuasicoherent]`)
the prover must, for each basic open `D(r) ≤ q.X i`, turn the *slice* presentation
`q.presentation i : (M.over (q.X i)).Presentation` into `IsIso ((M|_{D(r)}).fromTildeΓ)` on
`Spec R_r`. Two walls were reported: (1) "Mathlib `AlgebraicGeometry/Modules/` has no
restriction-to-open functor on `(Spec R).Modules`, no `over`↔pullback bridge, no QC-preservation";
(2) even *stating* `q.presentation i` times out on the slice `IsRightAdjoint` instance. Is the
`over`-slice route the wrong shape? Does Mathlib already provide the transport / the gap1 result?

## Project artifact(s)
- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean:653` — `isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff_isLocalizedModule_restrict`
  (the reduction engine: gap1 ⇔ per-basic-open `IsLocalizedModule`; **built, axiom-clean**).
- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean:686` — `isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_presentation`
  (global-presentation endpoint; **built**).
- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean:730` — `exists_finite_basicOpen_cover_le_quasicoherentData`
  (finite basic-open cover refining `q.X`; **built**).
- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean:253` — `SheafOfModules.IsLocallyFreeOfRank`
  (**already uses `Scheme.Modules.pullback (U i).ι`** — i.e. the project already knows the geometric
  restriction functor exists).

## Mathlib facts established (pinned commit; project's `.lake` Mathlib)

Restriction / pullback of `(Spec R).Modules` — **obstacle #1 is factually wrong**:
- `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullback (f : X ⟶ Y) : Y.Modules ⥤ X.Modules`
  (`Modules/Sheaf.lean:167`), a left adjoint (`:180`), additive (`:183`).
- An entire **`Restriction` section** for open immersions (`Modules/Sheaf.lean:312–451`):
  `restrictFunctor f : Y.Modules ⥤ X.Modules` (`:319`, docstring "isomorphic to the pullback
  functor … but has better defeqs"), `restrict M f` (`:325`), `restrict_obj : Γ(M.restrict f, U)
  = Γ(M, f ''ᵁ U)` (`:328`), `restrictFunctorIsoPullback : restrictFunctor f ≅ pullback f`
  (`:371`), `restrictFunctorComp` (`:392`), `restrictStalkNatIso` (restriction commutes with
  stalks, `:424`). This IS "restriction of a `SheafOfModules` along an open immersion of schemes."

Presentation transport — **exists as a general idiom**:
- `SheafOfModules.Presentation.map (F) (η : F.obj (unit R) ≅ unit S) : Presentation (F.obj M)`
  for any `F` with `[PreservesColimitsOfSize F]` (`Quasicoherent.lean:179`); plus
  `Presentation.mapGenerators/mapRelations` (`:159,166`) and `Presentation.ofIsIso` (`:132`).
  `pullback`/`restrictFunctor` are left adjoints (preserve colimits) and send `unit ↦ unit`, so
  `Presentation.map` applies to them.

The abstract slice `over` (what `QuasicoherentData` actually carries):
- `SheafOfModules.over M X := (pushforward (𝟙 _)).obj M : SheafOfModules (R.over X)`
  (`PushforwardContinuous.lean:53`) — the **abstract Grothendieck-slice** restriction over the
  *site object* `X`, NOT the geometric `Scheme.Modules.restrict`. No Mathlib lemma connects the two.
- `QuasicoherentData` (`Quasicoherent.lean:201`) carries `presentation i : (M.over (X i)).Presentation`;
  `IsQuasicoherent` (`:249`) is `Nonempty QuasicoherentData` — **the only Mathlib access to local
  presentations is through the slice `over`.** There is no slice-free extractor.

The gap1 result itself — **absent**, exactly as in `quot-qcoh-affine-globalization.md`:
- `isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff : IsIso M.fromTildeΓ ↔ (tilde.functor R).essImage M` (`Tilde.lean:340`);
- `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation` needs a **global** `M.Presentation` (`Tilde.lean:398`);
- comment `Tilde.lean:310`: "We will later show that the essential image is exactly quasi-coherent
  modules" — i.e. QCoh = essImage is **not** in Mathlib.
- `presentationTilde` (`Tilde.lean:373`) + `(tilde M).IsQuasicoherent` (`:394`) — the EASY direction
  (any `tilde N` has a global presentation, since any module is `coker` of frees).

QC-preservation:
- The ONLY QC lemma is `IsQuasicoherent.of_coversTop` (`Quasicoherent.lean:377`): local QC ⟹ global
  QC. There is **no** "pullback/over preserves `IsQuasicoherent`".

The Mathlib template for slice presentation transport:
- `QuasicoherentData.bind` (`Quasicoherent.lean:360–375`) transports a presentation across an
  **iterated slice** via `(((D i.1).presentation i.2).map e.inverse (.refl _)).ofIsIso …`, where
  `e := pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence (Over.iteratedSliceEquiv …)`
  (`PushforwardContinuous.lean:305`, `CategoryTheory/Sites/Over.lean:471–489`). It carries
  `#adaptation_note /-- After nightly-2026-02-23 we need this to avoid timeouts. -/` and
  `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false` — **Mathlib's own slice-presentation
  transport fights exactly the timeout the prover hit, and tames it with `set_option`.**

The iterated-slice equivalences (`Sites/Over.lean:469–489`) only relate `(J.over X).over f` to
`J.over (composite)` — slices-of-slices. They do **not** bridge the opens-site slice `J.over U` to
the *small Zariski site of the open subscheme* `U.toScheme`. That bridge is absent.

## Decisions identified

### Decision A: is there a restriction-to-open functor on `(Spec R).Modules`? (obstacle #1)
- **Mathlib idiom**: yes — `Scheme.Modules.pullback` and the open-immersion `restrictFunctor`
  (`Modules/Sheaf.lean:167,319`), with `restrict_obj` defeq, `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`,
  `restrictStalkNatIso`. The project *already* uses `Scheme.Modules.pullback (U i).ι` in
  `IsLocallyFreeOfRank` (`QuotScheme.lean:255`).
- **Gap**: the obstacle-#1 claim is **wrong**. These functors live in `Modules/Sheaf.lean`, which the
  prover apparently treated as out of the `{Presheaf, Sheaf, Tilde}` set. No parallel functor should
  be built.
- **Verdict**: PROCEED (use `Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor`/`pullback`; do not hand-roll).

### Decision B: is the `M.over (q.X i)` / `q.presentation i` slice route the wrong shape?
- **Mathlib idiom**: For transporting a `QuasicoherentData` presentation, the slice route **is** the
  canonical idiom — `QuasicoherentData.bind` is the worked template (`Presentation.map e.inverse`
  + `pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence (Over.iteratedSliceEquiv g)` + `.ofIsIso`). `IsQuasicoherent`
  is *defined* only via slice `QuasicoherentData`, so there is **no slice-free** way to pull a local
  presentation out of `[M.IsQuasicoherent]` (answer to directive Q2(i): NO).
- **The timeout is not structural.** Mathlib's `bind` defeats the very same
  `(sheafToPresheaf (J.over …)).IsRightAdjoint`/`HasSheafify` synthesis blow-up with
  `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false` (+ heartbeat headroom). The prover hit it
  because those options were not in force.
- **Gap**: divergent-equivalent — the route is right, but it is *not where the difficulty lives*, and
  pushing slice-site manipulation all the way to `Spec R_r` by hand is the wrong amount of work.
- **Verdict**: PROCEED on the transport *as a sub-step*, but minimize slice contact (see Decision C).

### Decision C: the genuinely missing ingredient — the `over U ↔ Spec R_r` bridge
- **Mathlib idiom**: NONE. To apply `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation` (which needs
  `M : (Spec S).Modules` and a *geometric* presentation) we must identify the abstract slice
  `M.over (D r)` — an object of `SheafOfModules ((Spec R).ringCatSheaf.over (D r))` — with the
  geometric `(Spec R_r).Modules` and `M.restrict (D r).ι` across `D(r) ≅ Spec R_r`. Mathlib has the
  pieces but not the bridge: `Scheme.Hom.opensFunctor` (`OpenImmersion.lean:87`), the
  basicOpen↔`Spec R_r` isos (`AffineScheme.lean:566–572`, `isLocalization_basicOpen`), but **no**
  equivalence `J.over U ≃ Opens(U.toScheme)` of sites carrying `R.over U ≅ U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf`,
  hence no `M.over U ≅ M.restrict U.ι`.
- **Gap**: divergent-with-cost / missing — this single bridge is the load-bearing piece the prover's
  plan silently assumed. Without it, "transport the slice presentation to a presentation of
  `M|_{D(r)}` on `Spec R_r`" cannot even be *typed*.
- **Verdict**: NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL — build `overRestrictIso` (named ingredient).

### Decision D: the section-localization descent (the real keystone, per iter-026)
- **Mathlib idiom**: NONE for the local→global step. Even granting `IsIso ((M|_{D(r)}).fromTildeΓ)`
  on each `Spec R_r`, that is section-localization of `M|_{D(r)}` over `R_r`; assembling the
  **global**-over-`R` statement `IsLocalizedModule (powers f) (Γ(M,⊤) → Γ(M,D f))` that the engine
  consumes requires the sheaf-equalizer + flat-localization descent (Hartshorne II.5.3 / Stacks
  01HA): `IsLocalization.flat`, finite cover, `M.isSheaf`. The plan's appeal to
  `isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_presentation` does NOT close this — that lemma needs a *global*
  presentation of `M` on `Spec R`, which is not produced by per-`D(r)` data.
- **Gap**: divergent-and-underestimated — this is genuine multi-step descent, the same irreducible
  content flagged in `quot-qcoh-affine-globalization.md`.
- **Verdict**: NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL — this is the keystone, not the transport.

## Recommendation

The slice route is **not the wrong shape for the transport sub-step**, and obstacle #1 is wrong
(`Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor`/`pullback` exist). But framing gap1 as "transport a presentation,
then `isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_presentation`" mislocates the work: the load-bearing missing
pieces are the **`over U ↔ Spec R_r` bridge (C)** and the **section-localization descent (D)**.
Build gap1 in this order, keeping slice contact to a single tamed lemma:

1. **`overRestrictIso` (C, the bridge).** For `U : X.Opens` of a scheme `X`, build
   `M.over U ≅ (restrictFunctor U.ι).obj M` (equivalently land it through
   `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`), via the site equivalence `J.over U ≃ Opens(U.toScheme)` induced by
   `U.ι.opensFunctor` carrying `R.over U ≅ U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf`. State and prove it with
   `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false` (Mathlib's own `bind` incantation) to
   dodge the `IsRightAdjoint`/`HasSheafify` synthesis timeout. This is the ONE lemma that must touch
   the slice site; everything downstream is geometric.

2. **Per-affine local-tilde (P1).** For `r ∈ t` (from `exists_finite_basicOpen_cover_le_quasicoherentData`)
   with `D(r) ≤ q.X i`: take `q.presentation i : (M.over (q.X i)).Presentation`; transport to the
   smaller open with `Presentation.map` along the **iterated-slice** functor (template:
   `QuasicoherentData.bind`, `Quasicoherent.lean:360`); push through `overRestrictIso` and the
   `D(r) ≅ Spec R_r` iso (`Presentation.map` along `Scheme.Modules.pullback`) to land
   `(M' : (Spec R_r).Modules).Presentation`, then `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation`.

3. **Section-localization descent (D, the keystone).** From "M is locally tilde on the finite
   basic-open cover {D(r)}", prove `∀ f, IsLocalizedModule (powers f) (Γ(M,⊤) → Γ(M,D f))` via the
   sheaf equalizer over the finite cover + `IsLocalization.flat` (localization is exact and commutes
   with the finite limit). This is the genuine content; it is reused by gap2.

4. **Assemble.** Feed (3) into the existing `isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff_isLocalizedModule_restrict`
   (`QuotScheme.lean:653`) — already axiom-clean — to land `IsIso M.fromTildeΓ`.

Blueprint these four as separate `\lean{…}` obligations so a mathlib-build prover gets the right
shape; (C) and (D) are the two genuinely new lemmas, (P1)/(4) are Mathlib glue. Note the standing
project memory `quot-quasicoherentdata-slice-transport-wall` should be updated: the wall is *not*
"no restriction functor" (there is one) but "no `over U ↔ Spec R_r` bridge + the descent is the
keystone", and the slice timeout is tamed by `backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false`.

=== quot-predicates ===
# Analogy: QUOT predicate shapes — Mathlib idiom alignment

## Mode
api-alignment

## Slug
quot-predicates

## Iteration
004

## Question
For the two project-side predicates needed by the Quot functor and Grassmannian
foundations — (1) "coherent sheaf with schematic support proper over the base" and
(2) "rank-`d` locally free `SheafOfModules`" — determine for each whether Mathlib
already supplies it, the recommended Mathlib-aligned Lean shape, the exact Mathlib
declarations to build on, and the cost of a parallel choice.

## Project artifact(s)
- `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex` `def:quot_functor` (L422-494) — the
  Quot functor classifies `T`-flat coherent quotients `q : E_T ↠ F` whose **schematic
  support is proper over `T`**; `def:grassmannian_scheme` (L504-564) classifies rank-`d`
  **locally free** quotients; `Grass(V,d)` is *defined* as `Quot^{d,O_S}_{V/S/S}`.
- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean` — `hilbertPolynomial`, `QuotFunctor`,
  `Grassmannian`, `Grassmannian.representable` are all typed `sorry` skeletons keyed on
  `X.Modules = SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf`; "coherent" is meant to be encoded as
  `[F.IsQuasicoherent]` + `[F.IsFiniteType]` (per `thm:generic_flatness`), but the
  proper-support and rank-`d`-local-freeness conditions are not yet expressed.

## Decisions identified

### Decision 1a: How to express "schematic support of a `Scheme.Modules` object"

- **Mathlib idiom**: Mathlib has the *target* closed-subscheme-of-vanishing machinery but
  NOT the support of a sheaf of modules.
  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.IdealSheafData` — structure of ideal sheaves
    (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/IdealSheaf/Basic.lean:65`).
  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.IdealSheafData.ofIdeals (I : ∀ U : X.affineOpens, Ideal Γ(X,U))`
    (`…/IdealSheaf/Basic.lean:104`) — builds an ideal sheaf from an affine-local ideal
    assignment.
  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.ker (f : X ⟶ Y) : IdealSheafData Y`
    (`…/IdealSheaf/Basic.lean:689`), defined `ofIdeals fun U ↦ RingHom.ker (f.app U).hom`
    — the **exact template** for an annihilator ideal sheaf.
  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.IdealSheafData.subscheme : Scheme`
    (`…/IdealSheaf/Subscheme.lean:452`) and
    `…IdealSheafData.subschemeι : I.subscheme ⟶ X` (`…/Subscheme.lean:472`); the inclusion
    is `IsPreimmersion` + `QuasiCompact` (`…/Subscheme.lean:484,488`), i.e. a closed
    immersion onto `I.support`.
  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.IdealSheafData.support : Closeds X`
    (`…/IdealSheaf/Basic.lean:327`); `range_subschemeι : Set.range I.subschemeι = I.support`
    (`…/Subscheme.lean:493`).
  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal (Z : Closeds X) : IdealSheafData X`
    (`…/IdealSheaf/Basic.lean:563`) — the reduced induced structure on a closed set.
  - Ring-level support primitives that the affine-local assignment is built from:
    `Module.annihilator R M` (`Mathlib/RingTheory/Ideal/Colon.lean`, used throughout) and
    `Module.support R M : Set (PrimeSpectrum R)` (`Mathlib/RingTheory/Support.lean`).
  - **Absent**: any `support`/`annihilatorIdealSheaf` for `SheafOfModules` /
    `Scheme.Modules`. A whole-tree grep for a sheaf-level module support returns nothing;
    the only `support` in `AlgebraicGeometry` is `IdealSheafData.support` and `Hom.support_ker`.
- **Project's current path**: not yet expressed (the `.lean` skeleton drops the condition).
This is a substantial transport lemma. Let me explore the key Mathlib API signatures before drafting.

#adaptation_note /-- After nightly-2026-02-23 we need this to avoid timeouts. -/
/-- Given an cover `X` and a quasicoherent data for `M` restricted onto each `Mᵢ`, we may glue them
into a quasicoherent data of `M` itself. -/
noncomputable def QuasicoherentData.bind {R : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}}
    (M : SheafOfModules.{u} R) {I : Type u}
    (X : I → C) (hX : J.CoversTop X) (D : Π i, QuasicoherentData (M.over (X i))) :
    M.QuasicoherentData where
  I := Σ i, (D i).I
  X ij := ((D ij.1).X ij.2).left
  coversTop Y := J.transitive (hX Y) _ fun Z f ⟨i, ⟨g⟩⟩ ↦
      J.superset_covering ((Sieve.functorPushforward_ofObjects_le _ _ _).trans
      (Sieve.ofObjects_mono fun i' ↦ by aesop)) ((D i).coversTop (.mk g))
  presentation i :=
    letI e := pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence (Over.iteratedSliceEquiv ((D i.1).X i.2))
      (S := (R.over _).over _) (R := R.over _) (𝟙 _) (𝟙 _)
      (by ext : 2; exact R.1.map_id _) (by ext : 2; exact R.1.map_id _)
    (((D i.1).presentation i.2).map e.inverse (.refl _)).ofIsIso
      (e.fullyFaithfulFunctor.preimageIso
      (by exact e.counitIso.app ((M.over (X i.1)).over ((D i.1).X i.2)))).hom

lemma IsQuasicoherent.of_coversTop {R : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}}
    (M : SheafOfModules.{u} R) {I : Type u}
    (X : I → C) (hX : J.CoversTop X) [∀ i, IsQuasicoherent (M.over (X i))] :
    IsQuasicoherent M :=
  (QuasicoherentData.bind M X hX fun _ ↦
    IsQuasicoherent.nonempty_quasicoherentData.some).isQuasicoherent

end bind

end SheafOfModules

We introduce `unitToPushforwardObjUnit φ` which is the morphism
in the category `SheafOfModules S` which corresponds to `φ`, and
show that the adjoint morphism
`pullbackObjUnitToUnit φ : (pullback.{u} φ).obj (unit S) ⟶ unit R`
is an isomorphism when `F` is a final functor.

of modules `free I` to `free I`, see `pullbackObjFreeIso` and
`freeFunctorCompPullbackIso`.

@[expose] public section

universe v v₁ v₂ u₁ u₂ u

open CategoryTheory Limits

namespace SheafOfModules

variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type u₂} [Category.{v₂} D]
  {J : GrothendieckTopology C} {K : GrothendieckTopology D} {F : C ⥤ D}
  {S : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}} {R : Sheaf K RingCat.{u}}
  [Functor.IsContinuous F J K]
  (φ : S ⟶ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj R)

/-- The canonical map from the (global) sections of a sheaf of modules
to the (global) sections of its pushforward. -/
@[simps]
def pushforwardSections {M : SheafOfModules.{v} R} (s : M.sections) :
    ((pushforward φ).obj M).sections where
  val _ := s.val _
  property _ := s.property _

variable (M) in
lemma bijective_pushforwardSections [F.Final] :
    Function.Bijective (pushforwardSections φ (M := M)) :=
  Functor.bijective_sectionsPrecomp _ _

variable [J.HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u})]
  [K.HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u})]

/-- The canonical morphism `unit S ⟶ (pushforward.{u} φ).obj (unit R)`
of sheaves of modules corresponding to a continuous map between ringed sites. -/
noncomputable def unitToPushforwardObjUnit : unit S ⟶ (pushforward.{u} φ).obj (unit R) where
  val.app X := ModuleCat.homMk ((forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat).map (φ.hom.app X)) (fun r ↦ by
    ext m
    exact ((φ.hom.app X).hom.map_mul _ _).symm)
  val.naturality f := by
    ext
    exact ConcreteCategory.congr_hom (φ.hom.naturality f) _

lemma unitToPushforwardObjUnit_val_app_apply {X : Cᵒᵖ} (a : S.obj.obj X) :
    (unitToPushforwardObjUnit φ).val.app X a = φ.hom.app X a := rfl

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
lemma pushforwardSections_unitHomEquiv
    {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (f : unit R ⟶ M) :
    pushforwardSections φ (M.unitHomEquiv f) =
      ((pushforward φ).obj M).unitHomEquiv
        (unitToPushforwardObjUnit φ ≫ (pushforward φ).map f) := by
  ext X
  have := unitToPushforwardObjUnit_val_app_apply φ (X := X) 1
  dsimp at this ⊢
  simp +instances [this, map_one]
  rfl

variable [(pushforward.{u} φ).IsRightAdjoint]

/-- The canonical morphism `(pullback.{u} φ).obj (unit S) ⟶ unit R`
of sheaves of modules corresponding to a continuous map between ringed sites. -/
noncomputable def pullbackObjUnitToUnit :
    (pullback.{u} φ).obj (unit S) ⟶ unit R :=
  ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction.{u} φ).homEquiv _ _).symm (unitToPushforwardObjUnit φ)

@[simp]
lemma pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_symm_unitToPushforwardObjUnit :
    ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction.{u} φ).homEquiv _ _).symm (unitToPushforwardObjUnit φ) =
      pullbackObjUnitToUnit φ := rfl

@[simp]
lemma pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_pullbackObjUnitToUnit :
    (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction.{u} φ).homEquiv _ _ (pullbackObjUnitToUnit φ) =
      unitToPushforwardObjUnit φ :=
  Equiv.apply_symm_apply _ _

instance [F.Final] : IsIso (pullbackObjUnitToUnit φ) := by
  rw [isIso_iff_coyoneda_map_bijective]
  intro M
  rw [← ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction.{u} φ).homEquiv _ _).bijective.of_comp_iff',
    ← (unitHomEquiv _).bijective.of_comp_iff']
  convert (bijective_pushforwardSections φ M).comp (unitHomEquiv _).bijective
  ext f : 1
  dsimp
  rw [pushforwardSections_unitHomEquiv, EmbeddingLike.apply_eq_iff_eq,
    Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right,
    pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_pullbackObjUnitToUnit]

variable [HasWeakSheafify J AddCommGrpCat.{u}] [HasWeakSheafify K AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
  [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
  [K.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{u}] [F.Final]

/-- The pullback of a free sheaf of modules is a free sheaf of modules. -/
noncomputable def pullbackObjFreeIso (I : Type u) :
    (pullback φ).obj (free I) ≅ free I :=
  (asIso (sigmaComparison _ _)).symm ≪≫
    Sigma.mapIso (fun _ ↦ asIso (pullbackObjUnitToUnit φ))

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
lemma pullback_map_ιFree_comp_pullbackObjFreeIso_hom {I : Type u} (i : I) :
    (pullback φ).map (ιFree i) ≫ (pullbackObjFreeIso φ I).hom =
      pullbackObjUnitToUnit φ ≫ ιFree i := by
  simp [pullbackObjFreeIso, ιFree]

@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
lemma pullbackObjFreeIso_hom_naturality {I J : Type u} (f : I → J) :
    (pullback φ).map (freeMap f) ≫ (pullbackObjFreeIso φ J).hom =
      (pullbackObjFreeIso φ I).hom ≫ freeMap f :=
  Cofan.IsColimit.hom_ext (isColimitCofanMkObjOfIsColimit (pullback φ) _ _
    (isColimitFreeCofan (R := S) I)) _ _ (fun i ↦ by simp [← Functor.map_comp_assoc])

variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type u₂} [Category.{v₂} D]
  {J : GrothendieckTopology C} {K : GrothendieckTopology D} (eqv : C ≌ D)
  {S : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}} {R : Sheaf K RingCat.{u}}
  [Functor.IsContinuous eqv.functor J K]
  [Functor.IsContinuous eqv.inverse K J]
  (φ : S ⟶ (eqv.functor.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj R)
  (ψ : R ⟶ (eqv.inverse.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} K J).obj S)
  (H₁ : Functor.whiskerRight (NatTrans.op eqv.counit) R.obj =
    ψ.hom ≫ eqv.inverse.op.whiskerLeft φ.hom)
  (H₂ : φ.hom ≫ eqv.functor.op.whiskerLeft ψ.hom ≫
    Functor.whiskerRight (NatTrans.op eqv.unit) S.obj = 𝟙 S.obj)

/-- If `e : C ≌ D`, then the pushforwards along `e.functor` and `e.inverse` forms an equivalence. -/
noncomputable
def pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence : SheafOfModules R ≌ SheafOfModules S where
  functor := pushforward.{v} φ
  inverse := pushforward.{v} ψ
  unitIso :=
    letI := CategoryTheory.Functor.isContinuous_comp eqv.inverse eqv.functor K J K
    (pushforwardId _).symm ≪≫ pushforwardNatIso _ eqv.counitIso ≪≫
      pushforwardCongr (by ext1; simpa) ≪≫ (pushforwardComp _ _).symm
  counitIso :=
    letI := CategoryTheory.Functor.isContinuous_comp eqv.functor eqv.inverse J K J
    pushforwardComp _ _ ≪≫ pushforwardNatIso _ eqv.unitIso ≪≫
      pushforwardCongr (by ext1; simpa) ≪≫ pushforwardId _
  functor_unitIso_comp :=
    (pushforwardPushforwardAdj eqv.toAdjunction φ ψ H₁ H₂).left_triangle_components

    {J : GrothendieckTopology C} {K : GrothendieckTopology D} {F : C ⥤ D}

  rw [← isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso _ (sheafToPresheaf _ _)]
  rw [NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app]

import Mathlib
import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.QuotScheme
open CategoryTheory Limits AlgebraicGeometry SheafOfModules
open TopologicalSpace Topology

private theorem isIso_unitToPushforwardObjUnit_of_isIso {C : Type u} [Category.{u} C]
    {D : Type u} [Category.{u} D]
    {J : GrothendieckTopology C} {K : GrothendieckTopology D} {Fu : C ⥤ D}
    {S : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}} {Rr : Sheaf K RingCat.{u}} [Fu.IsContinuous J K]
    (ψ : S ⟶ (Fu.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj Rr)
    [J.HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u})]
    [K.HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u})]
    [IsIso ψ] :
    IsIso (SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit.{u} ψ) := by
  haveI hmap : IsIso ((sheafToPresheaf J RingCat).map ψ) := inferInstance
  rw [NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app] at hmap
  rw [← isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso _ (SheafOfModules.toSheaf _)]
  rw [← isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso _ (sheafToPresheaf _ _)]
  rw [NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app]
  intro V
  haveI hiso : IsIso (ψ.hom.app V) := hmap V
  haveI : IsIso ((forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat).map (ψ.hom.app V)) := inferInstance
  convert this using 1

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — slice-to-geometric presentation transport (gap1, P1)

This section builds the geometric milestone of the gap1 per-element transport
`lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent` (P1): a `SheafOfModules.Presentation` of the
abstract Grothendieck-slice restriction `M.over U` is transported, across the slice-to-geometric
bridge `overRestrictPullbackIso` (gap1, C), into a `SheafOfModules.Presentation` of the *geometric*
restriction `(Scheme.Modules.pullback U.ι).obj M = U.ι^* M` on the open subscheme `U.toScheme`.

The load-bearing ingredient is the unit-iso `overRestrictUnitIso`: the slice-to-geometric
equivalence functor `(overRestrictEquiv U).functor` (definitionally a `SheafOfModules.pushforward`
along the equivalence-of-sites inverse with the *identity* ring comparison) sends the structure-sheaf
module `unit` to `unit`. This is exactly the `F.obj (unit R) ≅ unit S` datum that
`SheafOfModules.Presentation.map` consumes; once it is in hand, `Presentation.map` + `Presentation.ofIsIso`
(across `overRestrictPullbackIso`) realise the transport. The unit-iso rests on the general fact
`isIso_unitToPushforwardObjUnit_of_isIso'`: the canonical map `unit S ⟶ (pushforward ψ).obj (unit R)`
is an isomorphism whenever the ring-sheaf comparison `ψ` is (here `ψ = 𝟙`).

Mathlib (at the pinned commit) supplies `SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit` and proves it iso
only under a finality hypothesis on the site functor (`PullbackFree.lean`); the
`IsIso ψ ⟹ IsIso (unitToPushforwardObjUnit ψ)` route used here, and the whole slice transport, are
project-local. -/

namespace AlgebraicGeometry

open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace Topology

/-- **`unitToPushforwardObjUnit` is an isomorphism when the ring-sheaf comparison is.**

For a continuous functor `F` of sites and a morphism of ring sheaves
`ψ : S ⟶ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous …).obj R` that is an isomorphism, the canonical comparison
`unitToPushforwardObjUnit ψ : unit S ⟶ (pushforward ψ).obj (unit R)` is an isomorphism. Its component
on each object is `(forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat).map (ψ.hom.app _)`, an isomorphism since `ψ` is;
the conclusion follows by the reflect-isomorphism functors `SheafOfModules.toSheaf` and
`sheafToPresheaf` together with `NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app`.

Project-local: Mathlib proves `unitToPushforwardObjUnit` iso only under a finality hypothesis on `F`
(`SheafOfModules.PullbackFree`); this `IsIso ψ`-driven form is the one the slice-to-geometric unit-iso
`overRestrictUnitIso` (gap1, P1) consumes (with `ψ = 𝟙`). -/
private theorem isIso_unitToPushforwardObjUnit_of_isIso' {C : Type u} [Category.{u} C]
    {D : Type u} [Category.{u} D]
    {J : GrothendieckTopology C} {K : GrothendieckTopology D} {Fu : C ⥤ D}
    {S : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}} {Rr : Sheaf K RingCat.{u}} [Fu.IsContinuous J K]
    (ψ : S ⟶ (Fu.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj Rr)
    [J.HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u})]
    [K.HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u})]
    (hψ : IsIso ψ) :
    IsIso (SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit ψ) := by
  haveI := hψ
  haveI hmap : IsIso ((sheafToPresheaf J RingCat).map ψ) := inferInstance
  rw [NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app] at hmap
  rw [← isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso _ (SheafOfModules.toSheaf _)]
  rw [← isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso _ (sheafToPresheaf _ _)]
  rw [NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app]
  intro V
  haveI hiso : IsIso (ψ.hom.app V) := hmap V
  haveI : IsIso ((forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat).map (ψ.hom.app V)) := inferInstance
  convert this using 1

namespace Scheme.Modules

variable {X : Scheme.{u}}

/-- **The slice-to-geometric equivalence functor sends `unit` to `unit`** (gap1, P1).

For an open `U ⊆ X`, the functor of the slice-to-geometric equivalence `overRestrictEquiv U`
(definitionally `SheafOfModules.pushforward` along `(Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse` with the
identity ring comparison) carries the sliced structure-sheaf module `unit (O_X.over U)` to the
structure-sheaf module `unit (U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf)` of the open subscheme. This is the
`F.obj (unit R) ≅ unit S` datum consumed by `SheafOfModules.Presentation.map` in
`overRestrictPresentation`. Project-local. -/
noncomputable def overRestrictUnitIso (U : X.Opens) :
    (overRestrictEquiv U).functor.obj (SheafOfModules.unit (X.ringCatSheaf.over U)) ≅
      SheafOfModules.unit U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf := by
  unfold overRestrictEquiv
  dsimp only [Equivalence.symm_functor]
  refine (@asIso _ _ _ _ (SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit
      (F := (Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse) (J := Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U)
      (S := U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf) (R := X.ringCatSheaf.over U)
      (ObjectProperty.homMk (𝟙 _)))
    (isIso_unitToPushforwardObjUnit_of_isIso' _ ?hpsi)).symm
  exact inferInstanceAs (IsIso (𝟙 U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf))

/-- **Slice presentation ⟹ geometric-restriction presentation** (gap1, P1).

Given a sheaf of modules `M` on `X`, an open `U ⊆ X`, and a `SheafOfModules.Presentation` of the
abstract Grothendieck-slice restriction `M.over U`, there is a `SheafOfModules.Presentation` of the
*geometric* restriction `(pullback U.ι).obj M = U.ι^* M` on the open subscheme `U.toScheme`. The
transport is `Presentation.map` along the slice-to-geometric equivalence functor (using the unit-iso
`overRestrictUnitIso`) followed by `Presentation.ofIsIso` across the bridge `overRestrictPullbackIso`
(gap1, C).

This closes the slice-touching step of the gap1 per-element transport
`lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent` (P1): with `U = q.X i` and
`P = q.presentation i` it produces a global presentation of `U.ι^* M`; the remaining geometric step
restricts further to a basic affine `D(r) ≅ Spec R_r` and concludes via
`isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation`. Project-local. -/
noncomputable def overRestrictPresentation (U : X.Opens) (M : X.Modules)
    (P : (M.over U).Presentation) : ((Scheme.Modules.pullback U.ι).obj M).Presentation :=
  SheafOfModules.Presentation.ofIsIso.{u} (overRestrictPullbackIso U M).hom
    (SheafOfModules.Presentation.map.{u} P (overRestrictEquiv U).functor (overRestrictUnitIso U))

end Scheme.Modules

end AlgebraicGeometry

end AlgebraicGeometry

equivalence functor `(overRestrictEquiv U).functor` (definitionally a `SheafOfModules.pushforward`
along the equivalence-of-sites inverse with the *identity* ring comparison) sends the structure-sheaf
module `unit` to `unit`. This is exactly the `F.obj (unit R) ≅ unit S` datum that
`SheafOfModules.Presentation.map` consumes; once it is in hand, `Presentation.map` + `Presentation.ofIsIso`
(across `overRestrictPullbackIso`) realise the transport. The unit-iso rests on the general fact
`isIso_unitToPushforwardObjUnit_of_isIso'`: the canonical map `unit S ⟶ (pushforward ψ).obj (unit R)`
is an isomorphism whenever the ring-sheaf comparison `ψ` is (here `ψ = 𝟙`).

Mathlib (at the pinned commit) supplies `SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit` and proves it iso
only under a finality hypothesis on the site functor (`PullbackFree.lean`); the
`IsIso ψ ⟹ IsIso (unitToPushforwardObjUnit ψ)` route used here, and the whole slice transport, are
project-local. -/

1021	section SliceGeometricPresentation
1022	
1023	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace Topology
1024	
1025	/-- **`unitToPushforwardObjUnit` is an isomorphism when the ring-sheaf comparison is.**
1026	
1027	For a continuous functor `F` of sites and a morphism of ring sheaves
1028	`ψ : S ⟶ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous …).obj R` that is an isomorphism, the canonical comparison
1029	`unitToPushforwardObjUnit ψ : unit S ⟶ (pushforward ψ).obj (unit R)` is an isomorphism. Its
1030	component on each object is `(forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat).map (ψ.hom.app _)`, iso since `ψ` is;
1031	the conclusion follows by the reflect-isomorphism functors `SheafOfModules.toSheaf` and
1032	`sheafToPresheaf` together with `NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app`.

noncomputable def overRestrictPresentation (U : X.Opens) (M : X.Modules)
    (P : (M.over U).Presentation) : ((Scheme.Modules.pullback U.ι).obj M).Presentation :=
  SheafOfModules.Presentation.ofIsIso.{u} (overRestrictPullbackIso U M).hom
    (SheafOfModules.Presentation.map.{u} P (overRestrictEquiv U).functor (overRestrictUnitIso U))
input.new_string:
noncomputable def overRestrictPresentation (U : X.Opens) (M : X.Modules)
    (P : (M.over U).Presentation) : ((Scheme.Modules.pullback U.ι).obj M).Presentation :=
  SheafOfModules.Presentation.ofIsIso.{u} (overRestrictPullbackIso U M).hom
    (SheafOfModules.Presentation.map.{u} P (overRestrictEquiv U).functor (overRestrictUnitIso U))

/-- **Geometric restriction to a cover member is globally presented** (gap1, P1).

For a sheaf of modules `M` on `X` with quasi-coherence data `q` and an index `i`, the geometric
restriction `(pullback (q.X i).ι).obj M = (q.X i).ι^* M` of `M` to the open subscheme
`(q.X i).toScheme` admits a `SheafOfModules.Presentation`. It is `overRestrictPresentation` applied
to the slice presentation `q.presentation i : (M.over (q.X i)).Presentation` supplied by the
quasi-coherence datum.

This is the per-cover-member output that feeds the affine descent of the gap1 transport
`lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent` (P1): for `D(r) ≤ q.X i` one further restricts this
presentation to the basic affine `D(r) ≅ Spec R_r` and concludes via
`isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation`. The heartbeat headroom tames the slice-site
`IsRightAdjoint`/`HasSheafify` synthesis blow-up that `Presentation.map` triggers across the
equivalence functor (the same `backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false` incantation Mathlib's own
`QuasicoherentData.bind` uses). Project-local. -/
set_option maxHeartbeats 2000000 in
set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 800000 in
-- slice-site `IsRightAdjoint`/`HasSheafify` synthesis through `Presentation.map` is expensive
set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
noncomputable def presentationPullbackιOfQuasicoherentData (M : X.Modules)
    (q : M.QuasicoherentData) (i : q.I) :
    ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i))).obj M).Presentation :=
  overRestrictPresentation (q.X i) M (q.presentation i)

noncomputable def overRestrictPresentation (U : X.Opens) (M : X.Modules)
    (P : (M.over U).Presentation) : ((Scheme.Modules.pullback U.ι).obj M).Presentation :=

# AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean

## Summary
- **Declarations added (4, all axiom-clean `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`):**
  - `isIso_unitToPushforwardObjUnit_of_isIso'` (private helper, line 1037)
  - `Scheme.Modules.overRestrictUnitIso` (line 1069)
  - `Scheme.Modules.overRestrictPresentation` (line 1095)
  - `Scheme.Modules.presentationPullbackιOfQuasicoherentData` (line 1118)
- **Declarations blocked (1):** the assigned target
  `Scheme.Modules.isIso_fromTildeΓ_restrict_basicOpen` — NOT added (would require the affine descent
  step below; no sorry left behind).
- **Sorry count across file:** before 4 → after 4 (the 4 pre-existing protected stubs at lines 123,
  161, 198, 225; I added **zero** sorries). Module builds GREEN (`lake build
  AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.QuotScheme`, 8317 jobs).

This iteration **closed the slice-touching crux** of gap1 P1 — the part the route analogy
(`analogies/quot-gap1-transport.md`, Decision C) flagged as "the ONE lemma that must touch the slice
site; everything downstream is geometric." With `presentationPullbackιOfQuasicoherentData` the
project now has, for every cover member `q.X i`, a genuine **global** `SheafOfModules.Presentation`
of the geometric restriction `(pullback (q.X i).ι).obj M` on the open subscheme `(q.X i).toScheme`.

## isIso_unitToPushforwardObjUnit_of_isIso' (line 1037) — RESOLVED, axiom-clean
- **Statement:** for a continuous site functor `F` and a ring-sheaf comparison
  `ψ : S ⟶ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous …).obj R` with `IsIso ψ`, the canonical
  `SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit ψ : unit S ⟶ (pushforward ψ).obj (unit R)` is an iso.
- **Approach:** reflect through `SheafOfModules.toSheaf` then `sheafToPresheaf`
  (both `ReflectsIsomorphisms`), `NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app`, and the component over `V` is
  `(forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat).map (ψ.hom.app V)` — iso because `ψ` is (functors preserve isos);
  `convert … using 1`.
- **Why project-local:** Mathlib (`SheafOfModules/PullbackFree.lean`) proves
  `unitToPushforwardObjUnit`/`pullbackObjUnitToUnit` iso only under an `F.Final` hypothesis. This
  `IsIso ψ`-driven form is what the equivalence-functor unit-iso needs (with `ψ = 𝟙`).

## Scheme.Modules.overRestrictUnitIso (line 1069) — RESOLVED, axiom-clean
- **Statement:** `(overRestrictEquiv U).functor.obj (unit (X.ringCatSheaf.over U)) ≅ unit
  U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf`.
- **Approach:** `unfold overRestrictEquiv; dsimp only [Equivalence.symm_functor]` exposes the functor
  as `SheafOfModules.pushforward ψ₀` with `ψ₀ = ObjectProperty.homMk (𝟙 _)` (the identity ring
  comparison) along `(Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse`; then
  `(asIso (unitToPushforwardObjUnit ψ₀)).symm` via the helper above.
- **Key elaboration trick (do NOT lose this):** the morphism `ψ₀` cannot be hand-spelled (its type
  carries the `↥↑U` vs `↥U` site coercion and a giant `pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence` term).
  Pin it by supplying `(F := (Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse)`,
  `(J := Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U)`, `(S := U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf)`,
  `(R := X.ringCatSheaf.over U)` to `unitToPushforwardObjUnit`, and pass the `IsIso` instance
  **positionally** through `@asIso _ _ _ _ _ (helper _ ?hpsi)`. The residual
  `IsIso (ObjectProperty.homMk (𝟙 _))` closes by `inferInstanceAs (IsIso (𝟙 U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf))`
  (homMk of `𝟙` is defeq to `𝟙` across the rfl ring identification).

## Scheme.Modules.overRestrictPresentation (line 1095) — RESOLVED, axiom-clean
- **Statement:** `(M.over U).Presentation → ((pullback U.ι).obj M).Presentation`.
- **Body:** `Presentation.ofIsIso.{u} (overRestrictPullbackIso U M).hom
  (Presentation.map.{u} P (overRestrictEquiv U).functor (overRestrictUnitIso U))`.
- **Universe gotcha (do NOT lose this):** `Presentation.map`/`Presentation.ofIsIso` default their
  module universe to `0`, producing a `Presentation.{0,0,0}` ≠ expected `.{u,u,u}` mismatch. Fix by
  the explicit `.{u}` annotation on **both** (`Presentation.ofIsIso.{u}`, `Presentation.map.{u}`) and
  by calling them by full name (`SheafOfModules.Presentation.map.{u} P …`, not `P.map.{u}` which is
  rejected because `P` is a local).

## Scheme.Modules.presentationPullbackιOfQuasicoherentData (line 1118) — RESOLVED, axiom-clean
- **Statement:** `(M : X.Modules) (q : M.QuasicoherentData) (i : q.I) → ((pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι
  (q.X i))).obj M).Presentation`.
- **Body:** `overRestrictPresentation (q.X i) M (q.presentation i)`.
- **Type gotcha:** `q.X i : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X` (the *site* object), and `.ι` is
  `Scheme.Opens.ι`; project it as `Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i)` (the two `Opens` types are defeq but
  `q.X i`'s elaborated type forces the `TopologicalSpace.Opens` head, blocking dot-notation `.ι`).
- **Heartbeat headroom (REQUIRED):** carries
  `set_option maxHeartbeats 2000000`, `set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 800000`,
  `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false`. Without these the
  `(sheafToPresheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥↑(q.X i)) AddCommGrpCat).IsRightAdjoint` /
  `HasSheafify` synthesis through `Presentation.map` times out — the exact blow-up Mathlib's own
  `QuasicoherentData.bind` tames with the same `respectTransparency false` incantation (confirmed by
  PROGRESS standing note and the analogy).

## Scheme.Modules.isIso_fromTildeΓ_restrict_basicOpen (the assigned target) — NOT ADDED
The full P1 target reduces, with the above in hand, to a **purely geometric affine-descent step** (no
more slice-site contact). The route is now concrete and uses `overRestrictPresentation` a *second*
time, so the slice timeout machinery is not re-encountered for the unit-iso (the final identification
is along an **iso** of schemes, whose `Opens.map` is `Final`, so Mathlib's
`SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit` `IsIso` instance applies directly — no new pushforward unit-iso
needed).

**Recommended completion recipe (next iter):**
1. Let `N := (pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i))).obj M`, with `PN := presentationPullbackιOfQuasicoherentData M q i : N.Presentation` on `Z := (q.X i).toScheme`.
2. Let `W : Z.Opens` be the open of `Z` corresponding to `D(r)` (`D(r) ≤ q.X i`). Produce the slice
   presentation `PNW : (N.over W).Presentation` from the **global** `PN` via
   `SheafOfModules.Presentation.map.{u} PN (SheafOfModules.pushforward (𝟙 (Z.ringCatSheaf.over W))) (by rfl)`
   — this is exactly the `Presentation.quasicoherentData` construction
   (`Quasicoherent.lean:314`), and needs **no** new unit-iso (the comparison is `𝟙`, iso `by rfl`).
3. `PW := overRestrictPresentation W N PNW : ((pullback W.ι).obj N).Presentation` on `W.toScheme`.
   (Re-uses the lemma built this iter; carry the same three `set_option`s.)
4. `W` is affine (`W ≅ D(r)`, a basic open of `Spec R`; `IsAffineOpen.basicOpen`/`isLocalization_basicOpen`),
   so `W.toScheme ≅ Spec (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away r))` via `IsAffineOpen.isoSpec`. Transport
   `PW` across this **iso** with `Presentation.map.{u} PW (pullback isoSpec.hom) η_iso`, where `η_iso`
   is `asIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit _)` — **available from Mathlib** because the iso's
   `Opens.map` is `Final` (`instance [F.Final] : IsIso (pullbackObjUnitToUnit φ)`,
   `PullbackFree.lean`). This lands a presentation on `Spec (Away r)`.
5. Define the target object `M|_{D(r)} := (that Spec (Away r))-module` (= `(pullback W.ι).obj N`
   transported, or equivalently `(pullback h).obj M` for `h : Spec (Away r) ⟶ Spec R` = `Spec.map`
   of `algebraMap R (Away r)`, identified via `Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp`). Conclude
   `IsIso ((M|_{D(r)}).fromTildeΓ)` by `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation`.

**Dead end — do NOT retry:** the *geometric* open-immersion route (restrict `N` directly to an affine
open of `Z` via `pullback j` for an open immersion `j : Spec A ⟶ Z`) is blocked: it needs
`(pullback j).obj (unit) ≅ unit`, i.e. `IsIso (pullbackObjUnitToUnit (j.toRingCatSheafHom))`, which
requires `Functor.Final (Opens.map j.base)` — **NOT** an instance for open immersions (verified:
`infer_instance` fails). The `overRestrictPresentation`-then-iso route (step 4 above) sidesteps this
because `overRestrictPresentation` realises the open restriction through the *pushforward*-based
equivalence (the slice bridge), and the only remaining `pullback`-unit-iso is along a scheme **iso**
(Final ✓).

## Why I stopped
**Partial progress.** 4 axiom-clean declarations added (the slice-touching crux of gap1 P1 closed):
`isIso_unitToPushforwardObjUnit_of_isIso'` (1037), `overRestrictUnitIso` (1069),
`overRestrictPresentation` (1095), `presentationPullbackιOfQuasicoherentData` (1118). The remaining
affine-descent step (5-step recipe above) is now a concrete geometric plan using
`overRestrictPresentation` twice + Mathlib's Final-based `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` + `isoSpec` +
`isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation`; it was not attempted in full this iteration due to budget after
the unit-iso elaboration fight. No comment-only approaches left unattempted in the code; the open
problem is fully scoped.
- **Informal agent:** unavailable (no LLM API key in env — confirmed by PROGRESS standing note).

## Needs blueprint entry
All 4 new non-private/private declarations are infrastructure with **no blueprint block yet**
(`lean_aux` nodes; `archon dag-query unmatched` will list the 3 public ones). The planner/reviewer
should add blocks restoring the 1-to-1 Lean↔blueprint correspondence:

1. **`AlgebraicGeometry.isIso_unitToPushforwardObjUnit_of_isIso'`** (PRIVATE — `\lean{}` pin resolves
   project-internally only). File: `QuotScheme.lean:1037`. Fact: `IsIso ψ ⟹ IsIso
   (unitToPushforwardObjUnit ψ)` via reflect-iso (`SheafOfModules.toSheaf`, `sheafToPresheaf`) +
   componentwise `forget₂`-preserves-iso. New dependency anchor for the gap1-C / P1 unit-iso.
2. **`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.overRestrictUnitIso`** (`def`). File: `QuotScheme.lean:1069`.
   Relies on: (1) above; the definitional `overRestrictEquiv.functor = pushforward (homMk (𝟙))`
   (gap1-C `def:over_restrict_equiv`). Natural blueprint home: a sub-lemma of
   `lem:over_restrict_iso` / `def:over_restrict_equiv` ("the slice equivalence functor preserves the
   structure-sheaf module").
3. **`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.overRestrictPresentation`** (`def`). File:
   `QuotScheme.lean:1095`. Relies on: `overRestrictUnitIso` (2), `overRestrictPullbackIso`
   (`lem:over_restrict_pullback_iso`), Mathlib `SheafOfModules.Presentation.map`/`.ofIsIso`
   (`lem:presentation_map_mathlib`). This is the slice-touching half of
   `lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent` (P1) — give it its own block `uses`-linked under
   P1.
4. **`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.presentationPullbackιOfQuasicoherentData`** (`def`). File:
   `QuotScheme.lean:1118`. Relies on: `overRestrictPresentation` (3),
   `SheafOfModules.QuasicoherentData` (`lem:quasicoherentData_*`),
   `Scheme.Modules.pullback`/`Scheme.Opens.ι`. The per-cover-member presentation feeding P1's affine
   descent.

---
name: sheafofmodules-slice-presentation-transport-tricks
description: Elaboration tricks for transporting SheafOfModules.Presentation across the QuotScheme slice-to-geometric bridge (gap1 P1).
metadata:
  type: reference
---

Hard-won elaboration tricks for the gap1 P1 presentation transport in
`AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean` (closed axiom-clean iter-033). Verified against the pinned
Mathlib; reuse for the remaining affine-descent step.

- **`overRestrictEquiv U` functor is a pushforward.** `unfold overRestrictEquiv; dsimp only
  [Equivalence.symm_functor]` exposes `(overRestrictEquiv U).functor = SheafOfModules.pushforward ψ₀`
  with `ψ₀ = ObjectProperty.homMk (𝟙 _)` along `(Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse`.
- **unit-iso via `IsIso ψ`, not Final.** Mathlib's `unitToPushforwardObjUnit`/`pullbackObjUnitToUnit`
  are iso only under `F.Final`. For the slice equivalence (ψ = 𝟙, iso) prove `IsIso
  (unitToPushforwardObjUnit ψ)` directly: reflect through `SheafOfModules.toSheaf` then
  `sheafToPresheaf` (both `ReflectsIsomorphisms`), `NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app`, component is
  `(forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat).map (ψ.hom.app V)` (iso since ψ is), `convert … using 1`.
- **Can't hand-spell ψ₀** (its type has the `↥↑U` vs `↥U` site coercion + giant
  `pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence` term). Pin via explicit `(F := (Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse)
  (J := Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) (S := U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf) (R := X.ringCatSheaf.over U)`
  args to `unitToPushforwardObjUnit`, and pass the `IsIso` positionally through `@asIso _ _ _ _ _ …`.
  Residual `IsIso (ObjectProperty.homMk (𝟙 _))` closes by `inferInstanceAs (IsIso (𝟙 …))`.
- **`Presentation.map`/`.ofIsIso` default module universe to 0** → `Presentation.{0,0,0}` mismatch.
  Annotate `.{u}` on both AND call by full name `SheafOfModules.Presentation.map.{u} P …` (dot
  `P.map.{u}` is rejected: `P` is a local var).
- **`q.X i : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X`** (the site object), so `.ι` (which is `Scheme.Opens.ι`)
  needs `Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i)`; the two `Opens` types are defeq but the elaborated head blocks dot
  notation.
- **Slice synthesis timeout** (`(sheafToPresheaf …).IsRightAdjoint`/`HasSheafify` through
  `Presentation.map`): needs `set_option maxHeartbeats 2000000`, `synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 800000`,
  `backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false` (same incantation as Mathlib `QuasicoherentData.bind`).
- **Open-immersion pullback unit-iso is a DEAD END:** `IsIso (pullbackObjUnitToUnit
  j.toRingCatSheafHom)` needs `Functor.Final (Opens.map j.base)`, NOT an instance for open
  immersions. Route the affine descent through `overRestrictPresentation` (pushforward-based) + a
  final `pullback`-along-an-**iso** (Final ✓) instead. See [[quot-gap1-transport]] and the handoff in
  `task_results/AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.md`.
